commit.message:
Add agent tools, SQL sanitizer, and v1 API

Introduce a new agent_tools module with agent-ready functions (query_ocean_data, proximity, trends, depth profile, DB stats) and a TOOL_MANIFEST for LLM tool discovery. Add a standalone sql_sanitizer module enforcing read-only SQL (SELECT/CTE-only, keyword checks, no stacked statements/comments, LIMIT cap) and convenience sanitize/raise wrapper. Integrate safety into sql_builder (apply sanitizer to every generated query) and add versioned API v1 endpoints in app.py (/api/v1/query, /api/v1/tools, /api/v1/validate-sql) with caching, row caps and error handling. Add CONTRIBUTING.md and update README.md to document the developer API, agent integration, and safety-first design. Minor defensive changes: graceful sanitizer fallback, structured JSON responses, and descriptive error/reporting for blocked queries.

commit.message:
Task 14: Vertex-color pipeline and primitives

Implement three vertex-color VkPipelines (triangle-list, triangle-strip,
line-list) sharing a single pipeline layout with 64-byte push constants
(mat4 projection) and vec2+vec4 vertex input matching Renderer::Vertex.

- createVertexColorPipelines(): creates all three topology pipelines using
  vertcolor.vert/frag SPIR-V shaders, alpha blending, dynamic viewport/scissor
- drawTriangleStrip(): uploads caller vertices to dynamic buffer, draws with
  triangle-strip pipeline
- drawLines(): same with line-list pipeline
- drawCircle(): generates triangle-list fill (32 segments, fan→individual
  triangles) + optional triangle-strip outline ring
- drawRoundedRect(): generates triangle-list fill from 4-corner-arc perimeter
  + optional triangle-strip outline ring, matching GLRenderer output
- Cleanup of all three pipelines + layout in cleanupVulkan()

fix: replace hard weight clip with soft cap + monotonicity guard

The prescription system hard-clipped weights to the historical max
(get_max_recent_entered_weight), which prevented progressive overload
and produced absurd prescriptions when the curve solved for heavier
weights (e.g., 200 lb x 14 reps @ RPE 9 after doing 200 lb x 13 @ RPE 8).

- prescribe_next_set: monotonicity guard bumps weight if clipping would

- plan_progressive_sets: same soft cap + monotonicity guard treatment
- detect_inflection: unchanged (already used 125% soft cap)

commit.message:
fix: preserve Next.js history state in URL sync effect

The game URL sync effect was calling replaceState(null, ...) on initial
room join, which destroyed the Next.js __N metadata on the /#room history
entry. When the user pressed back from /#room/playing, Next.js saw null
state and returned early from its popstate handler without calling
beforePopState — so the forfeit dialog never appeared.

Fix: only replaceState when transitioning from game to lobby (URL still
has /playing suffix), and preserve window.history.state so the Next.js
routing metadata stays intact.

Co-authored-by: takasaki404 <193405421+takasaki404@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(auth): wrap auth commands in block_in_place to avoid nested runtime panic

The OAuth device code flow uses reqwest::blocking::Client, which creates
an internal Tokio runtime. When called from within the #[tokio::main]
async context, dropping that nested runtime on a worker thread panics
with 'Cannot drop a runtime in a context where blocking is not allowed'.

Wrap the auth command dispatch in tokio::task::block_in_place() so the
blocking I/O (OAuth HTTP calls and dialoguer prompts) can execute safely
on the current worker thread.

commit.message:
Fix Chromium video freeze on workspace switch

The root cause is Hyprland stopping frame callbacks for windows on
invisible workspaces, which starves Chromium's video decode pipeline
on NVIDIA Wayland. Fix by adding a render_unfocused window rule for
Chromium and raising render_unfocused_fps from 15 to 60.

Revert the ineffective Chromium backgrounding flags from the previous
attempt — the issue is compositor-side, not browser-side.

commit.message:
fix: remove invalid --target-port flag — CLI arg never existed

ROOT CAUSE: 'az cognitiveservices agent create --target-port 8088' returns
'ERROR: unrecognized arguments: --target-port 8088' (exit code 2). This
flag does NOT exist in the Azure CLI cognitiveservices extension.

IMPACT: Every deploy since inception has failed at the PRIMARY CLI create
path, falling through to the SDK fallback (deploy_agent.py). The SDK
creates agent versions but cannot configure the Container App networking.
The Container App's ingress was never properly set up, causing the
persistent RequestTimedOut / client_disconnect errors.

FIX:
- Remove --target-port 8088 from CLI agent create (flag doesn't exist)
- Add agent delete before create to force fresh Container App provisioning
- Port 8088 auto-configured from Dockerfile EXPOSE directive
- Remove invalid safety-net create that also used --target-port
- Keep SDK fallback path for resilience

CI evidence (run #131): Both CLI create commands failed with exit code 2.
Smoke test: 5/5 attempts timed out at 100s (client_disconnect).

fix(ci): switch macOS to universal2 on macos-latest; fix aarch64 server binary linker (#43)

macos-13 (Intel) has been retired by GitHub — x86_64 macOS jobs were
immediately cancelled. The previous aarch64 server binary build used
--target aarch64-apple-darwin which triggered PyO3 cross-compile
detection even on a native aarch64 runner, causing unresolved Python
symbols at link time.

Changes:
- Replace x86_64 + aarch64 matrix with a single universal2 build on
  macos-latest; one wheel covers both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.
- Build server binary natively (no --target) so PyO3 uses the
  platform-native code path and resolves the Python framework correctly.
  The bundled binary is aarch64; Intel Macs run it via Rosetta 2.

Agent: Interface Segregation Sweep (#8391)

* Agent: Interface Segregation Sweep

* refactor: split CodemodEngine into role-focused interfaces (ISP)

Split the monolithic CodemodEngine interface (10 members spanning semantic
access, codemod execution, rename operations, workspace editing, and cache
management) into five cohesive interfaces:

- CodemodSemanticProvider: read-only semantic analyzer access
- CodemodTransformExecutor: file-transforming codemod execution
- CodemodRenameOperations: rename validate/plan/execute lifecycle
- CodemodWorkspaceEditor: workspace edit application
- CodemodCacheController: query cache invalidation

CodemodEngine becomes a composite extending all five interfaces, preserving
backwards compatibility. Consumers updated to depend on the minimal interface:

- naming-convention-codemod: narrowed from CodemodEngine to specific
  intersections (CodemodSemanticProvider & CodemodRenameOperations, etc.)
- Test mock ValidationDelayEngine: removed 4 unused stub methods by
  implementing only CodemodSemanticProvider & CodemodRenameOperations

Added ISP assignability tests verifying the structural contracts.
